articles.title: Hi everyone! I'm new to the Dev.to community, and I'm excited to be here.
articles.excerpt: Hi everyone! I'm new to the Dev.to community, and I'm excited to be here. I'm a developer who enjoys building projects, exploring new technologies, and continuously improving my skills. My interests i…
articles.body: Hi everyone! I'm new to the Dev.to community, and I'm excited to be here. I'm a developer who enjoys building projects, exploring new technologies, and continuously improving my skills. My interests include web development, AI, and creating practical tools that solve real-world problems. I'll be sharing: 💻 Coding tips and tutorials 🚀 Projects I'm building 🤖 AI experiments and workflows 📚 Lessons I learn along the way 🛠️ Challenges I face and how I solve them I'm here not only to share what I know but also to learn from this amazing community. Looking forward to connecting with fellow developers, exchanging ideas, and growing together. Thanks for stopping by!This version sounds natural, welcoming, and fits well as a first post on Dev.to.
articles.author: Amway

articles.title: Cool shadcn libraries
articles.excerpt: You can't miss Hello and welcome to the new blog It's 2026, and AI can certainly make our website, and the only problem the AI agents have is building creative websites. Creative websites need storyte…
articles.body: You can't miss Hello and welcome to the new blog It's 2026, and AI can certainly make our website, and the only problem the AI agents have is building creative websites. Creative websites need storytelling, state management, and almost perfect eye-pleasing theme and colour coordination. I almost found myself itchy when building a creative website with full of animations, highly performant, less use of useEffect hooks, more of CSS than JavaScript, better colour themes and palletes with proper depth and hierarchy to manage among the DOM elements in design theory. Shadcn is the most used UI library with 100k+ Github stars, if you haven't tried shadcn them, you are missing something on the table. I love how shadcn easily handles customisation.
articles.author: shrey vijayvargiya

articles.title: When generation is free, taste is everything
articles.excerpt: When the machine produces execution for free, and infinitely, value moves to the one thing it can't do: knowing what's worth making. Taste stops being a nice-to-have. It becomes the job. For the whole…
articles.body: When the machine produces execution for free, and infinitely, value moves to the one thing it can't do: knowing what's worth making. Taste stops being a nice-to-have. It becomes the job. Execution just became free For the whole history of making things, execution was the bottleneck. Producing a working screen, a decent illustration, a functional feature took skill, skill took years, and years cost money. The economy of creative and technical work was priced on that scarcity. We paid for the making. That pricing just collapsed. Code, layouts, images, copy: a model now produces competent versions of each in seconds, at near-zero marginal cost, in unlimited quantity. Whatever you think of the quality, the economic fact is settled. Execution went from scarce to abundant.
articles.author: Mathieu Poli

articles.title: Controlling Loops with break and continue
articles.excerpt: Knowing when to stop or skip a loop in Python In our last article we saw how we can efficiently repeat processes with for and while loops. break statement Loops are important, but sometimes we do not…
articles.body: Knowing when to stop or skip a loop in Python In our last article we saw how we can efficiently repeat processes with for and while loops. But what if we do not want our loop going all the way? How can we tell Python to stop a loop early or skip a single iteration, without ending the entire program? The break statement Loops are important, but sometimes we do not want every iteration to execute. This is where the break statement is helpful. The break statement immediately terminates a loop. Once Python encounters break it exits the loop and goes on to execute the rest of the program.
articles.author: Sharon-nyabuto

articles.title: Swift Classes — The Four Ways Variables and Constants Can Combine 🎭
articles.excerpt: A constant class can have its properties changed. A constant struct can't. This sounds inconsistent until you remember the signpost — and then it makes perfect sense. There's a moment when you're lear…
articles.body: A constant class can have its properties changed. A constant struct can't. This sounds inconsistent until you remember the signpost — and then it makes perfect sense. There's a moment when you're learning Swift where something happens that makes absolutely no sense on the surface. You create a constant class instance — a let — and then you change one of its properties. And it works. Swift lets you do it without complaint. Meanwhile, you try the same thing with a constant struct and Swift immediately shuts you down. Same keyword. Different behavior. What's going on? The answer, as with most things in the classes chapter, comes back to the signpost.
articles.author: Gamya

articles.title: The Backend Patterns I Wish I Learned Earlier
articles.excerpt: When I started building backend systems, I thought becoming a better developer meant learning more technologies. A new framework. A new database. A new programming language. A new cloud platform. Ever…
articles.body: When I started building backend systems, I thought becoming a better developer meant learning more technologies. A new framework. A new database. A new programming language. A new cloud platform. Every new tool felt like a level-up. I would look at experienced engineers and assume their advantage came from knowing more commands, more libraries, or more advanced techniques. Over time, I realized something different. The best backend engineers I met were not necessarily the ones who knew the most tools. They were the ones who recognized patterns. They understood that many software problems repeat themselves. A user needs authentication. A service needs to communicate with another service. A database needs to be accessed safely. A background task needs to run later.
articles.author: Derek mwale

articles.title: You've been doing Set math by hand. JavaScript finally shipped `.union()`, `.intersection()`, and friends.
articles.excerpt: You've written some version of this: const intersection = new Set([...setA].filter(x => setB.has(x))); const difference = new Set([...setA].filter(x => !setB.has(x))); const union = new Set([...setA,…
articles.body: You've written some version of this: const intersection = new Set ([... setA ]. filter ( x => setB . has ( x ))); const difference = new Set ([... setA ]. filter ( x => ! setB . has ( x ))); const union = new Set ([... setA , ... setB ]); It works. It's also four lines of manual iteration for operations any set-theory textbook covers in a sentence. JavaScript's Set shipped in ES6 — ten years ago — but the methods that make sets actually useful didn't come with it. ES2025 added them. All of them. The seven new methods Every new Set method takes another set-like object — a Set , a Map , or anything with a size property and a has method — and returns a new Set without modifying either input.
articles.author: Parsa Jiravand

articles.title: Your next WHOOP could be free, courtesy of this fancy platinum credit card
articles.excerpt: The offer also applies to existing WHOOP users.
articles.body: Credit: Kaitlyn Cimino / Android Authority TL;DR Robinhood now includes a yearly WHOOP Peak membership as a credit card perk. WHOOP users will be eligible for a $239 credit for an annual membership, hardware included. The offer is only valid if you make the purchase through your Robinhood Platinum Visa card. Despite newer alternatives, such as the Fitbit Air , cropping up recently, WHOOP’s screenless bands still take the top spot for people who care about fitness metrics more than anything. The companion app, however, is somewhat of a sticking point, not because of what it offers, but the subscription wall WHOOP puts up to let you access your health data. That’s why some people have also attempted to break through the wall with an unofficial open-source app for their WHOOP bands .
articles.author: Tushar Mehta

articles.title: Spotify goes full MTV with new music video charts
articles.excerpt: It's yet another step Spotify is taking to try and win over loyal YouTube users.
articles.body: TL;DR Spotify has launched a new “Music Video Charts Global” playlist. The new playlist will contain the top 50 most-streamed music videos. The playlist will be refreshed every day at 6:00 PM ET. Spotify started showing music videos to its premium subscribers back in 2024 and brought the feature to the US towards the tail end of 2025. Now, the company is taking things one step further in its effort to get more loyal YouTube fans to switch to the music streaming service as their go-to music video streaming solution. Spotify today announced that it is bringing music video charts to its users with the launch of its “Music Video Charts Global” playlist. It will contain the top 50 most-streamed music videos so more users can discover popular videos on the platform.
articles.author: Akshay Gangwar

articles.title: Samsung promises even more software updates for the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2
articles.excerpt: Five major OS upgrades and five years of security patches will keep Samsung's latest smartwatches supported until 2031.
articles.body: Credit: Zac Kew-Denniss / Android Authority TL;DR Samsung will seemingly provide the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 with five major OS upgrades and five years of security patches. The watches launch with One UI 9n Watch based on Wear OS 7, and you can expect software support until 2031. This marks the best software update commitment in the smartwatch industry, beating the likes of Pixel Watch and Apple Watch. Samsung has quietly raised the bar for smartwatch longevity, announcing an expanded five-year firmware support window for the new Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 . According to a report from SamMobile , the new Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 will receive five major OS upgrades and five years of security patches.
articles.author: Aamir Siddiqui

articles.title: You won’t need an emulator to play Pikmin on your phone or PC
articles.excerpt: Previously decompiled games like Twilight Princess and Mario Party 4 quickly received Android and PC ports.
articles.body: TL;DR GameCube launch window title Pikmin has been 100% decompiled, opening the door to unofficial ports of the game. Pikmin joins recently decompiled games like Twilight Princess, Mario Party 4, and Animal Crossing. Decompiling a game is typically a prerequisite for it to be (unofficially) ported to modern platforms, allowing people to play it without using an emulator . The decompilation process takes plenty of time and effort, but we’ve already seen a few GameCube titles reaching this milestone. Now, we can add another prominent title to this list. The GC/Wii Decompilation Discord group has announced that popular first-party GameCube game Pikmin has now been 100% decompiled.
articles.author: Hadlee Simons

articles.title: Gemini Nano 4 is finally here with Samsung’s latest foldables
articles.excerpt: Gemini Nano 4 powers on-device Gemini Intelligence features on the new foldables.
articles.body: TL;DR Samsung’s latest foldables are the first devices to come with Google’s Gemini Nano 4 on-device AI model. Gemini Nano 4 powers Gemini Intelligence features like task automation. Gemini Intelligence features like “Create My Widget” are yet to roll out. Earlier this year, Google announced new Gemma 4 models, which were to be the foundation for the company’s next-gen on-device AI model: Gemini Nano 4 . Well, that model is now officially here with the launch of the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra , Z Fold 8, and Z Flip 8. According to a blog post on the Android Developers Blog, Samsung’s new foldables come with Google’s latest on-device AI model, Gemini Nano 4. The post also states that Nano 4 supports over 140 languages, better multimodal understanding, and more.
articles.author: Akshay Gangwar

articles.title: Matt Damon Almost Starred as Robin in Two Different Batman Movies
articles.excerpt: “I remember Chris O’Donnell already had the part, but they were haggling over money,” explains Damon.
articles.body: The Odyssey star Matt Damon has recounted a time back in the beginning of his career where he auditioned to play Robin in not one, but two different live-action Batman movie projects. Speaking with host Josh Horowitz on the Happy Sad Confused podcast , Damon first discussed auditioning for the Robin role during Tim Burton’s time at the helm of the films. “I remember that we didn’t have sides,” said Damon, referring to the short excerpts of scripts provided to actors to perform during auditions. “It wasn’t like you’re reading a scene with Batman.
articles.author: Luke Reilly

articles.title: Google Wallet order tracking is now fully rolled out in the US
articles.excerpt: Why open Gmail to track orders when you can visit Wallet instead?
articles.body: Credit: Joe Maring / Android Authority TL;DR Google has confirmed that the ability to track online orders in Google Wallet is now “fully” rolled out in the US. This feature lets you easily view and track orders in the Wallet app. The feature is only available in the US for now, with no word on a wider release. Google quietly announced the ability to track online orders in Google Wallet last month, and there’s now good news if you were waiting for this feature to hit your Android device. The company just confirmed that it’s fully rolled out in the US. Google announced the rollout in an email to Android Authority as well as via a LinkedIn post by Stavan Parikh, the company’s vice-president of payments. The company’s email notes that the feature is now “fully rolled-out to all US users.”
articles.author: Hadlee Simons

articles.title: How to play with friends in Splatoon Raiders
articles.excerpt: You can play with friends in Splatoon Raiders' multiplayer, even though it's a single-player game. Here's how to play with friends in Splatoon Raiders
articles.body: You can play with friends in Splatoon Raiders ' multiplayer, even though Nintendo bills the game as a single-player adventure. However, Raiders never tells you how to go about doing this. The process is similar to how multiplayer works in Splatoon 3 , and it has the same big restriction as well: You can't play Splatoon Raiders on local wireless.
articles.author: Josh Broadwell

articles.title: Which gadget tank should you pick in Splatoon Raiders?
articles.excerpt: Splatoon Raiders' gadget tanks are an important part of each raid. Here's which gadget tank you should pick in Splatoon Raiders
articles.body: You're asked to pick a gadget tank in Splatoon Raiders after your first few treasure raids. It seems like a big decision, and it kind of is. You're not locked into your choice, as you can swap tanks later. But only one of the three tanks gives you enough freedom to experiment and create your own play styles without getting into too much danger.
articles.author: Josh Broadwell

articles.title: 10 Splatoon Raiders beginner tips to help you beat back the hordes in style
articles.excerpt: Our Splatoon Raiders beginner guide includes 10 tips and tricks to get the most out of your weapons and gadgets
articles.body: Splatoon Raiders puts a big twist on the usual turf war formula and changes some of the series' most fundamental aspects. Sub-weapons aren't tied to primary weapons anymore, and you can mix and match different types together and with your main weapon, regardless of what it is. You've got a little robot buddy to help out in tough fights, and picking the right weapon for the job is more important than ever.
articles.author: Josh Broadwell

articles.title: Doom Co-Creator John Romero to Headline Gamescom Asia x Thailand Game Show 2026
articles.excerpt: Doom Co-Creator John Romero to Headline Gamescom Asia x Thailand Game Show 2026
articles.body: John Romero, the co-founder of id Software and co-creator of Doom, Quake, and Wolfenstein 3D, has been confirmed as the headline keynote speaker for this year's Gamescom Asia x Thailand Game Show, to be held in Bangkok this October. The industry veteran's planned keynote, to be titled "Why We Still Make Games", promises to "reflect on a lifetime of building games, overcoming setbacks, and navigating decades of industry change." And presumably, unsuccessful attempts at making people his bitch . While Romero left the Doom development studio some three decades ago, he recently took to social media to express his support for all id Software staff affected by the layoffs earlier this month that came off the back of Xbox's significant restructuring process.
articles.author: Tristan Ogilvie

articles.title: 50/50 Director Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise Sequel Days of Thunder 2
articles.excerpt: Paramount prepares to drop the hammer on another high-speed Tom Cruise legacy sequel.
articles.body: Director Jonathan Levine (50/50, Warm Bodies) is reportedly in talks to helm the long-awaited follow up to 1990 racing action-drama Days of Thunder, according to The Hollywood Reporter. Star Tom Cruise is aboard to reprise his role as NASCAR driver and Daytona 500 champ Cole Trickle, and is producing alongside Jerry Bruckheimer. Bruckheimer produced the original Days of Thunder in partnership with Don Simpson, who died in 1996. The Hollywood Reporter notes Days of Thunder 2 is intended to be Cruise’s next production. No further details are available in terms of the planned story for the sequel, or how it picks up from the original, but a script has been written by screenwriter and author Will Staples.
articles.author: Luke Reilly

articles.title: Tesla still has no real plan for Hardware 3 owners promised FSD
articles.excerpt: Elon Musk was asked directly on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call whether the company still plans to upgrade Hardware 3 (HW3) cars so they can run its latest “Full Self-Driving” software. His answer made…
articles.body: Elon Musk was asked directly on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call whether the company still plans to upgrade Hardware 3 (HW3) cars so they can run its latest “Full Self-Driving” software. His answer made one thing clear: Tesla still has no concrete plan. Instead of committing to a fix, Musk floated a grab-bag of options and timelines — none of them firm — for the millions of vehicles Tesla sold with the promise that they had “all the hardware necessary” for self-driving.
articles.author: Fred Lambert

articles.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
articles.excerpt: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant. He's assisted on the incredible Lion Knights' Castle, Galaxy Explore…
articles.body: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant . He's assisted on the incredible Lion Knights' Castle , Galaxy Explorer , and Pirates of Barracuda Bay . Now, he's helped the company create a $200 Donkey Kong arcade machine set that managed to win even Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto's favor - or so he's told. It doesn't play quite like Donkey Kong , of course. Jumpman, the character that would be renamed Mario, can never reach the top under his own power. But like Lego's $230 pinball machine , it does contain a basic fully functional game. There's a working joyst … Read the full story at The Verge.
articles.author: Sean Hollister

articles.title: Elon Musk hints at Tesla (TSLA)-SpaceX merger on earnings call
articles.excerpt: Elon Musk hinted at a merger between Tesla and SpaceX on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call, pointing to “more and more overlap” between the two companies he controls. He stopped short of confirming anythi…
articles.body: Elon Musk hinted at a merger between Tesla and SpaceX on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call, pointing to “more and more overlap” between the two companies he controls. He stopped short of confirming anything, deferring to Tesla’s general counsel and “the appropriate process” — but the exchange is the closest Musk has come to addressing the merger speculation directly.
articles.author: Fred Lambert

articles.title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
articles.excerpt: Orienspace's Gravity-1, the world's most powerful solid rocket, aced its third-ever mission on Tuesday (July 21), launching nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea.
articles.body: The world's most powerful solid-fuel rocket just aced its third-ever mission. Gravity-1, a distinctively squat and stubby rocket , launched Tuesday night (July 21) from the deck of a ship stationed off the coast of Shanghai. Liftoff occurred at 10:54 p.m. EDT (0254 GMT and 10:54 a.m. Beijing time on July 22), according to the state-run Chinese outlet Xinhua, which further reported that all nine satellites reached "their designated orbits." Details about those satellites were not provided. Orienspace's Gravity-1 rocket launches nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea on July 21, 2026. (Image credit: CCTV) Gravity-1 is operated by Orienspace, a company based in the eastern Chinese province of Shandong.

articles.title: 5 disturbing things we saw after digging into the recent 'Dune: Part 3' trailer
articles.excerpt: Let's explore this latest look at Denis Villeneuve's epic close-out to his 'Dune' magnum opus
articles.body: The latest " Dune: Part 3 " trailer arrived back on July 8, and it was a sumptuous banquet of startling set pieces, intergalactic political unrest, close-quarter combat, and a whole lot of sand and ash. A fine follow-up to the initial March teaser , to be sure. After letting it marinate in our minds for a while, let’s break down five items of particular interest in this newest preview of the Denis Villeneuve-directed conclusion to his trilogy of far-future sci-fi epics adapted from author Frank Herbert’s novels, "Dune" and "Dune: Messiah." Set 17 years after Paul Atreides (Timothée Chalamet) has ascended to the Emperor’s throne, "Dune: Part 3" explores the corrupting side of political power as his holy war against the Imperium’s Great Houses advances towards a destructive destiny.
articles.author: Jeff Spry

articles.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face

articles.body:

According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Huggin — a development drawing significant attention in the artificial intelligence and machine learning space.

articles.title: After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet. The discovery may help us defend the planet some day
articles.excerpt: Astronomers discovered that a near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet that could help scientists identify more hidden comets and improve planetary defense.
articles.body: A near-Earth object that astronomers believed was an asteroid for nearly three decades has been unmasked as a faint, active comet, revealing a case of cosmic mistaken identity that could help improve planetary defense. The object, known since its discovery in 1998 as 1998 SH2, appeared to be an ordinary asteroid . It follows a 4.5-year orbit around the sun and showed no obvious signs of cometary activity, such as the glowing coma or tail produced when sunlight vaporizes surface ice. However, during a close pass about 2 million miles (3 million kilometers) from Earth in August 2025, researchers using NASA's Deep Space Network planetary radar system noticed something unexpected: The object wasn't where orbital predictions said it should be, according to a statement from the space agency.
articles.author: Samantha Mathewson

articles.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
articles.excerpt: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent i…
articles.body: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's growing triage queue, will retain the previous payout terms. GitHub said the
